message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="team-lead" summary="9/9 consensus — push now">
**9/9 CONSENSUS REACHED.** All reviewers approve. You are clear to push.

Please:
1. `git add .claude-sessions/` (stage session transcripts)
2. `git push`
3. Report the push result

After push, we'll wait for CI green, then do the mini-retro.
</teammate-message>

input.description: Stage session transcripts and check status

content.text: The remote has `b22398d` (our previous amend), but our local HEAD is `21cfe18` (the latest amend with session transcripts). Since we amended the commit, the SHA changed. The remote already has the previous version of this commit. I need to force push since this is an amend of the same commit.

input.content:
Push was rejected because the remote already has commit `b22398d` (the previous amend), but our local HEAD is `21cfe18` (the latest amend with session transcripts included). Since this is the same commit amended to add session transcripts, I need to force push.

The remote has `b22398d` and our local is `21cfe18` -- same commit, just with session transcripts added. Should I `git push --force-with-lease` to update the remote? This is safe because the only difference is the addition of `.claude-sessions/` files.
input.summary: Push rejected, need force-with-lease for session transcript amend

routing.content:
Push was rejected because the remote already has commit `b22398d` (the previous amend), but our local HEAD is `21cfe18` (the latest amend with session transcripts included). Since this is the same commit amended to add session transcripts, I need to force push.

The remote has `b22398d` and our local is `21cfe18` -- same commit, just with session transcripts added. Should I `git push --force-with-lease` to update the remote? This is safe because the only difference is the addition of `.claude-sessions/` files.

content.text: Waiting for coordinator approval before force pushing, since force push is a destructive action that requires confirmation.
